text.p: parts of North East Pastoral district.
The State Emergency Service advises that people should:
* Don't drive, ride or walk through flood water;
* Keep clear of creeks and storm drains;
* Stay indoors, away from windows, while conditions are severe.

warning-info.text: The next Severe Weather Warning will be issued by 5:00 pm ACDT Wednesday.

text.p: Warnings are also available through TV and Radio broadcasts, the Bureau's website at www.bom.gov.au or call 1300 659 210. The Bureau and State Emergency Service would appreciate warnings being broadcast regularly.

forecast-period.text: Heavy rainfall across the North East Pastoral, easing later today.

text.p: Weather Situation: A rich tropical airmass extends over the centre of the country with a surface trough extending through northeast parts of the state. Meanwhile an upper trough is moving across eastern parts of SA. This combination is continuing to bring the risk of heavy rainfall to northeastern parts of the state today, but conditions are expected to ease from late this afternoon.

HEAVY RAINFALL which may lead to FLASH FLOODING is forecast for parts of the North East Pastoral district today. Six-hourly rainfall totals between 30 and 60 mm are possible, especially with thunderstorms.
INTENSE RAINFALL is no longer expected, with broader conditions expected to ease from the late afternoon.
Flood Watches and Warnings are current for the area. Please refer to https://www.bom.gov.au/weather-and-climate/warnings-and-alerts/ for more information.
Locations which may be affected include Moomba, Innamincka, the area east of Lake Frome and the Strzelecki Track south of Moomba.

HEAVY RAINFALL which may lead to FLASH FLOODING is forecast for parts of the North East Pastoral district today. Six-hourly rainfall totals between 30 and 60 mm are possible, especially with thunderstorms.
INTENSE RAINFALL is no longer expected, with broader conditions expected to ease from the late afternoon.
Flood Watches and Warnings are current for the area. Please refer to https://www.bom.gov.au/weather-and-climate/warnings-and-alerts/ for more information.
Locations which may be affected include Moomba, Innamincka, the area east of Lake Frome and the Strzelecki Track south of Moomba.
